verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its catalytic domain and SH2/SH3 domains face the cytoplasm. It is not displayed on the outer leaflet. Checking all 5 contextual buckets: (1) No documented cancer-cell ecto-pool on the outer leaflet — inner-leaflet anchorage persists in tumor cells. (2) No documented cell-death-induced outer-leaflet exposure in the literature. (3) No tissue-restricted germline/developmental outer-leaflet display. (4) No lysosomal-exocytosis route; LYN is not a lysosomal TM protein. (5) No wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the outer leaflet. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic (antibody/ADC/CAR-T) targets LYN's protein body from outside. It is inner-leaflet-anchored, not surface-accessible.
